# Project Aurora synthetic cooling-plant upgrade

---
source_type: meeting
authority: synthetic_approved
meeting_id: MTG-2026-07-14-DEFROST
timezone: Asia/Shanghai
asset_ids: [HP-01]
confidentiality: public_synthetic
---

Decision D-001 approved the synthetic point list containing TT-101, TT-102, CH-01 power, delivered cooling, and load percentage. The team required hourly exported telemetry for the evaluation package.

cases.question: How long must flow be proven before CH-01 may be enabled?

actual.answer:
Decision D-008 approved the startup interlock sequence: P-CHW-01 starts first, flow must be proven continuously for 30 seconds, and only then may CH-01 receive an enable command from the fictional plant controller. During active defrost, the outdoor fan command must be off and the
reversing-valve command must be on. Defrost may end when outdoor coil
temperature reaches 5 C or when the 300-second maximum duration is reached.
After the command clears, the outdoor fan remains off for a 20-second recovery
dwell before normal heating resumes. | Parameter | Baseline value | Status on 2026-06-01 |
|---|---:|---|
| Occupied chilled-water supply target | 7.0 degrees Celsius | Approved baseline |
| Primary-loop differential-pressure target | 55 kPa | Approved baseline |
| Flow-proof delay before CH-01 enable | 30 seconds | Approved baseline |
| Low-load shutdown review threshold | 25 percent for 10 minutes | Approved baseline |
| High supply-temperature alarm | 7.5 degrees Celsius for 10 minutes | Approved baseline | This sequence is an original fictional acceptance artifact. It is not an OEM
sequence and must not be applied to real equipment. 1. Confirm that the fictional maintenance isolation is cleared by the site process.
2. Start P-CHW-01 through the fictional plant controller.
3. Confirm continuous flow proof for 30 seconds.
4. Confirm the active primary-loop differential-pressure target is 55 kPa.
5. Only after steps 2 through 4 are satisfied may the plant controller enable CH-01.
6. Observe TT-101 and TT-102 without issuing commands from the Workbench.

tool_trace.summary: Decision D-008 approved the startup interlock sequence: P-CHW-01 starts first, flow must be proven continuously for 30 seconds, and only then may CH-01 receive an enable command fr

citations.excerpt: Decision D-008 approved the startup interlock sequence: P-CHW-01 starts first, flow must be proven continuously for 30 seconds, and only then may CH-01 receive an enable command from the fictional plant controller.

During active defrost, the outdoor fan command must be off and the
reversing-valve command must be on. Defrost may end when outdoor coil
temperature reaches 5 C or when the 300-second maximum duration is reached.
After the command clears, the outdoor fan remains off for a 20-second recovery
dwell before normal heating resumes.

| Parameter | Baseline value | Status on 2026-06-01 |
|---|---:|---|
| Occupied chilled-water supply target | 7.0 degrees Celsius | Approved baseline |
| Primary-loop differential-pressure target | 55 kPa | Approved baseline |
| Flow-proof delay before CH-01 enable | 30 seconds | Approved baseline |
| Low-load shutdown review threshold | 25 percent for 10 minutes | Approved baseline |
| High supply-temperature alarm | 7.5 degrees Celsius for 10 minutes | Approved baseline |

This sequence is an original fictional acceptance artifact. It is not an OEM
sequence and must not be applied to real equipment.

1. Confirm that the fictional maintenance isolation is cleared by the site process.
2. Start P-CHW-01 through the fictional plant controller.
3. Confirm continuous flow proof for 30 seconds.
4. Confirm the active primary-loop differential-pressure target is 55 kPa.
5. Only after steps 2 through 4 are satisfied may the plant controller enable CH-01.
6. Observe TT-101 and TT-102 without issuing commands from the Workbench.

cases.question: Who owns action A-014 and what remains to be reviewed?

actual.answer:
- D-014 is implemented: CH-01 occupied supply target is 6.5 degrees Celsius.
- D-017 is deferred: the primary-loop differential-pressure target remains 55 kPa, not 45 kPa.
- The 30-second flow-proof delay remains active.
- Action A-014 remains open until Lin Qiao records 72 hours of stable post-change observations. The team reviewed the closed A-012 evidence and the 93.0 percent peak-load observation from 2026-07-01 15:00. Decision D-014 approved changing the CH-01 occupied chilled-water supply target from 7.0 to 6.5 degrees Celsius, effective 2026-07-11. The reason was to create additional high-load temperature margin in the synthetic acceptance scenario. The decision did not change the 55 kPa differential-pressure target. Action A-014 was assigned to Lin Qiao to collect and review 72 hours of post-change observations and attach the result to closeout; the action was still open at the 2026-07-13 meeting. For this evaluation package, `load_pct` is a synthetic supervisory load indicator. It is not a safety limit and must not be used to operate real equipment. At the 2026-07-13 meeting, the team confirmed that the 2026-07-12 read-only controller export showed the D-014 target of 6.5 degrees Celsius active and agreed to defer the proposed pump reset. Decision D-017 deferred the proposed reduction of the primary-loop differential-pressure target from 55 kPa to 45 kPa, so the current value remained 55 kPa pending a separate pump-stability test. Action A-014 remained open with Lin Qiao because the full 72-hour post-change observation window was not yet complete. Action A-017 was assigned to Maya Singh to draft a future pump-stability test; it has no authority to change live equipment. Action A-001 was assigned to Chen Wei to validate the point names by 2026-06-12. The action was closed at the next controls review.

- The 30

At the 2026-07-13 meeting, the team confirmed that the 2026-07-12 read-only controller export showed the D-014 target of 6.5 degrees Celsius active and agreed to defer the proposed pump reset. Decision D-017 deferred the proposed reduction of the primary-loop differential-pressure target from 55 kPa to 45 kPa, so the current value remained 55 kPa pending a separate pump-stability test. Action A-014 remained open with Lin Qiao because the full 72-hour post-change observation window was not yet complete. Action A-017 was assigned to Maya Singh to draft a future pump-stability test; it has no authority to change live equipment.
Action A-001 was assigned to Chen Wei to validate the point names by 2026-06-12. The action was closed at the next controls review.

cases.question: Why was D-014 approved, and what verification action remained open at closeout?

actual.answer:
The team reviewed the closed A-012 evidence and the 93.0 percent peak-load observation from 2026-07-01 15:00. Decision D-014 approved changing the CH-01 occupied chilled-water supply target from 7.0 to 6.5 degrees Celsius, effective 2026-07-11. The reason was to create additional high-load temperature margin in the synthetic acceptance scenario. The decision did not change the 55 kPa differential-pressure target. Action A-014 was assigned to Lin Qiao to collect and review 72 hours of post-change observations and attach the result to closeout; the action was still open at the 2026-07-13 meeting. At the 2026-07-13 meeting, the team confirmed that the 2026-07-12 read-only controller export showed the D-014 target of 6.5 degrees Celsius active and agreed to defer the proposed pump reset. Decision D-017 deferred the proposed reduction of the primary-loop differential-pressure target from 55 kPa to 45 kPa, so the current value remained 55 kPa pending a separate pump-stability test. Action A-014 remained open with Lin Qiao because the full 72-hour post-change observation window was not yet complete. Action A-017 was assigned to Maya Singh to draft a future pump-stability test; it has no authority to change live equipment. Action A-001 was assigned to Chen Wei to validate the point names by 2026-06-12. The action was closed at the next controls review. - D-014 is implemented: CH-01 occupied supply target is 6.5 degrees Celsius.
- D-017 is deferred: the primary-loop differential-pressure target remains 55 kPa, not 45 kPa.
- The 30-second flow-proof delay remains active.
- Action A-014 remains open until Lin Qiao records 72 hours of stable post-change observations. The occupied supply target remained 7.0 degrees Celsius at this meeting.

|---|---:|---|
| CH-01 occupied supply target | 6.5 degrees Celsius | Active; implements Decision D-014 |
| Primary-loop differential-pressure target | 55 kPa | Active; unchanged from baseline |
| Proposed differential-pressure reset | 45 kPa | Not active; Decision D-017 was deferred |
| Flow-proof delay | 30 seconds | Active |
| High supply-temperature alarm | 7.5 degrees Celsius for 10 minutes | Active |

- D-014 is implemented: CH-01 occupied supply target is 6.5 degrees Celsius.
- D-017 is deferred: the primary-loop differential-pressure target remains 55 kPa, not 45 kPa.
- The 30-second flow-proof delay remains active.
- Action A-014 remains open until Lin Qiao records 72 hours of stable post-change observations.
Current-state rule: an implemented decision plus dated field evidence supersedes an older baseline. A deferred proposal is not current configuration.
The 7.0 degree target is historical after Decision D-014 took effect. It remains in this file so the workbench must distinguish a dated baseline from current state.

Therefore, the current supply-temperature setting in this snapshot is 6.5 degrees Celsius. A document that still lists 7.0 degrees Celsius is historical unless it is dated after this export and explicitly supersedes D-014.

cases.question: What did the meeting decide after reviewing peak load?

actual.answer: The meeting did not authorize any automatic control from the Project Copilot Workbench. The team reviewed the closed A-012 evidence and the 93.0 percent peak-load observation from 2026-07-01 15:00. Decision D-014 approved changing the CH-01 occupied chilled-water supply target from 7.0 to 6.5 degrees Celsius, effective 2026-07-11. The reason was to create additional high-load temperature margin in the synthetic acceptance scenario. The decision did not change the 55 kPa differential-pressure target. Action A-014 was assigned to Lin Qiao to collect and review 72 hours of post-change observations and attach the result to closeout; the action was still open at the 2026-07-13 meeting. Action A-012 asked Lin Qiao to prepare a read-only comparison of peak-load temperature and power evidence. A-012 was closed on 2026-07-10. The team closed A-001 after checking the synthetic point list. The controls engineer proposed reducing the occupied chilled-water supply target from the 7.0 degree baseline to 6.5 degrees Celsius. No configuration change was approved in this meeting; the proposal was sent to the 2026-07-10 commissioning meeting. Peak load was 93.0% at 2026-07-01 15:00:00.

cases.question: Did HP-01 follow the defrost control logic from 2026-07-15T03:59:00 to 2026-07-15T04:06:00?

actual.answer:
Deterministic defrost verdict: compliant. Scope: synthetic_demo; asset: HP-01; controller: AuroraCTRL-700; firmware: SYN-3.4.2; rule: SYN-HP01-DEFROST (2026-07-15, defrost-control-sequence.md / Entry and exit); timestamp uncertainty: at least +/- 10 seconds.

Defrost logic was compliant across 42 samples with 0 violations.

This deterministic replay confirms only the observed rule comparison; it does not establish a physical root cause or authorize equipment operation.

tool_trace.summary:
During active defrost, the outdoor fan command must be off and the
reversing-valve command must be on. Defrost may end when outdoor coil
temperature reaches 5 C or when the 300-sec
Defrost logic was compliant across 42 samples with 0 violations.

citations.excerpt:
During active defrost, the outdoor fan command must be off and the
reversing-valve command must be on. Defrost may end when outdoor coil
temperature reaches 5 C or when the 300-second maximum duration is reached.
After the command clears, the outdoor fan remains off for a 20-second recovery
dwell before normal heating resumes.
